name: Assist Agency - Vermilion Parish
description_short: ASSIST Agency provides non-profit financial assistance with rent, mortgage, and utility payments across three Louisiana parishes by appointment only.
description_long:
ASSIST Agency, Inc. is a non-profit organization serving three parishes in southwestern Louisiana: Acadia, Jeff Davis, and Vermilion. The organization operates with a mission to provide emergency financial assistance to qualified residents struggling with housing costs and utilities. The agency maintains a governance structure with a Board of Directors and publishes annual reports and strategic plans demonstrating organizational transparency and accountability to the communities it serves.

The primary services offered by ASSIST Agency are direct financial assistance with rent or mortgage payments and utility bills. The organization operates on an appointment-only basis and requires applicants to call their respective parish office to determine eligibility before receiving assistance. The Vermilion Parish office is located in Abbeville and can be reached at 337-898-9554. The agency maintains office hours Monday through Thursday from 8:00 AM to 4:30 PM and Friday from 8:00 AM to 12:00 PM.

What distinguishes ASSIST Agency is its localized, multi-parish approach to community assistance combined with its commitment to accessibility. The organization explicitly provides auxiliary aids and services for individuals with disabilities and maintains a TDD line. As a true non-profit free-help provider, the agency does not offer loans, credit repair services, or products that charge fees or interest. The board meets bi-monthly to ensure ongoing governance and community responsiveness.

The primary limitation is that assistance is available by appointment only and requires qualifying application, meaning not all applicants will receive help. The website provides limited detail about specific eligibility criteria, income thresholds, or assistance amount limits. This is a genuine non-profit community resource best suited for residents already living in the three served parishes who face acute housing or utility crises.
pros: Completely free assistance with no fees, interest charges, or loan repayment obligations
Serves three Louisiana parishes with dedicated local offices in each community
Equal Opportunity Employer/Program with accessible services for people with disabilities and TDD availability
Transparent governance with published annual reports and strategic plans available online
Long office hours (Monday-Thursday until 4:30 PM) with multiple contact methods including phone and email
Addresses root causes of financial stress (rent/mortgage/utilities) rather than charging for financial products
Board of Directors provides oversight and conducts bi-monthly meetings open to community awareness
cons: Appointment-only model creates barriers to immediate access for people in urgent housing crisis situations
Website provides no information about income limits, eligibility criteria, or maximum assistance amounts
Limited service area restricted to only three Louisiana parishes (Acadia, Jeff Davis, Vermilion)
No online application process or way to pre-screen eligibility before calling, requiring phone contact
No indication of wait times, approval rates, or how frequently applicants receive full versus partial assistance
best_for: Vermilion Parish residents facing acute rent, mortgage, or utility payment shortfalls
Low-to-moderate income households in Acadia or Jeff Davis parishes seeking emergency housing assistance
Individuals with disabilities who need accessible services and accommodation in Louisiana's Vermilion Parish area
People seeking genuinely free help without loan debt or credit implications
diagnosis: ASSIST Agency is best for Vermilion Parish residents who are already facing a specific rent, mortgage, or utility crisis and have time to call during business hours to schedule an appointment. The main caveat is that eligibility is not guaranteed, the website provides no transparent criteria for approval, and emergency assistance requires advance scheduling rather than same-day crisis response.
services: Rent payment assistance for qualified applicants
Mortgage payment assistance for qualified applicants
Utility payment assistance for qualified applicants
Services in Vermilion Parish (Abbeville office location)
Services in Acadia Parish (Crowley office location)
Services in Jeff Davis Parish (Jennings office location)
Accessibility services and auxiliary aids for people with disabilities
TDD/relay service for hearing impaired individuals
Community needs assessment and strategic planning
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
